title: Cyclobalanopsis obovatifolia
taxonomy: Cyclobalanopsis obovatifolia is classified within the family Fagaceae, which includes beech and oak trees. It belongs to the genus Cyclobalanopsis, a group of deciduous and evergreen trees native to East Asia. Specifically, C. obovatifolia is classified as a species within the genus Cyclobalanopsis. The plant is further categorized as a member of the order Fagales, a group of flowering plants that also includes walnuts and hickories. This classification is based on the plant's morphological and molecular characteristics.
uses: Cyclobalanopsis obovatifolia is a plant species known for its traditional medicinal uses in East Asian countries, particularly in China and Korea. The plant's leaves and bark are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. In traditional medicine, Cyclobalanopsis obovatifolia is also believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to protect against cell damage and promote overall well-being. The plant's extracts have been studied for their potential to inhibit the growth of certain microorganisms, making it a subject of interest for further research. However, more studies are needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal properties and potential applications.

These compounds have been shown to possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. The plant's flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, are known for their ability to scavenge free radicals and protect against oxidative stress. The tannins in the plant have been reported to possess astringent and anti-inflammatory effects.

intro: Cyclobalanopsis obovatifolia, also known as the Chinese oak tree, is a species of evergreen tree native to China and Southeast Asia, belonging to the beech family (Fagaceae). The leaves and bark of this plant have been used in traditional Chinese medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Cyclobalanopsis obovatifolia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the leaves and bark of Cyclobalanopsis obovatifolia are commonly prepared into teas, infusions, and decoctions to treat various health conditions, and are also used in combination with other herbs to enhance their effects.
